label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about why social networking sites are harmful to users according to the passage. The options are A, B, C, D. First, I need to go back to the text and find the key points. The passage starts by saying that social networking sites like Facebook, Twitter, and QQ are daily must-visits, but then it mentions they can increase risks of diseases. The main point here is about the negative effects. The passage states that despite their purpose, these sites make people more isolated. Dr. Aric Sigman is quoted saying that sending messages through sites can cause big side effects in the body's biological changes. Increased isolation affects the immune response and can lead to health issues like cancer, strokes, etc. Then it talks about how face-to-face meetings have decreased since the 1987 rise of electronic media. People prefer virtual meetings over in-person ones. Education uses online systems so kids don't leave their rooms, parents spend less time with kids, and shared dinners decrease while more people work online. Dr. Sigman's quote at the end says that electronic media reduces social interaction abilities if people are isolated. The idea is that social networks were meant to improve life but instead replace real interactions. Looking at the options: A) They fail to bring users the pleasure they want. The passage says the sites do give pleasure but also have risks. So A is incorrect. B) They don't bring users enough convenience. The text doesn't mention convenience being lacking; it's more about the negative consequences despite convenience. So B is probably not right. C) More isolation is caused by less direct communication. The passage directly connects increased isolation due to less face-to-face interaction. This is a key point made by Dr. Sigman and the study. So C seems correct. D) They have no effect on removing people's isolation. But the passage says they actually increase isolation, so D is incorrect because the sites aren't helping with isolation; they are causing more of it.
